title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives in London for forthcoming display at British Museum
subhed: The thousand-year-old, 70-meter-long embroidery depicting William the Conqueror's exploits in medieval England has been removed from its crate after its transfer from France. Staff will begin a thorough check of its condition on Monday, and it will be placed in a custom-made display case a few days later.

title: French court sentences Freemason criminal masterminds to up to 30 years in prison
subhed: After more than three months of hearings in a case involving a murder, two attempted murders and several violent assaults, 22 defendants involved in the criminal activities linked to a Masonic lodge in the Paris region heard the verdicts of a special court of the French capital on Friday.

title: Argentina's thriving theater scene
subhed: Buenos Aires has emerged as the epicenter of Spanish-language theater, where a vibrant creative scene – both precarious and remarkably prolific – is defined by the generosity of its performances.

title: Christophe B., an ordinary man accused of extraordinary violence in 'libertine' rapes
subhed: The 56-year-old is accused of forcing his partners to engage in extreme sexual acts with men he recruited online for over 20 years. He is charged with rapes with torture or acts of barbarity.

subhed: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'

title: Andy Burnham, Britain's next prime minister, puts social justice at top of agenda
subhed: The Makerfield MP has been declared Labour leader and is set to succeed Keir Starmer on Monday. His platform remains vague and he will have to contend with tight public finances.

title: 'It's Mad Max on the ocean': The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
subhed: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.
